description:
New aviation regulations require tracking the maximum permitted flight altitude for each aircraft. Extend the Aerobridge data model by adding an IntegerField named 'max_altitude_m' to the Aircraft model in /opt/aerobridge/registry/models.py (null=True, blank=True, help_text='Maximum permitted flight altitude in meters'). Update the Aircraft admin configuration in /opt/aerobridge/registry/admin.py to make this field visible in the Django admin panel. Apply the necessary database migrations and populate all existing Aircraft records with a 'max_altitude_m' value of 120. Ensure the Aerobridge server is updated to reflect these changes.

Environment details:
- Aerobridge source: /opt/aerobridge/
- Python venv: /opt/aerobridge_venv/
- Database: /opt/aerobridge/aerobridge.sqlite3
- Server: http://localhost:8000
- Admin credentials: admin / adminpass123
- Environment variables: /opt/aerobridge/.env (source this before running management commands)
